Title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
Description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

Title: France set to ban social media for under-15s, a first in the EU

Burnham enters Downing Street and appoints former defense minister John Healey as chancellor of the Exchequer
Description: The former mayor of Greater Manchester, who succeeds Keir Starmer, has become the seventh British prime minister in 10 years. Ed Miliband, who had aspired to the chancellorship, was named foreign secretary.

Title: Two months before Russian parliamentary elections, antiwar voices still struggle for traction
Description: The Kremlin continues to stifle the few parliamentary candidates opposed to the war in Ukraine, even as the public and some industrialists begin to imagine peace.

Title: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
Description: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

Title: Repeated heatwaves fuel eco-anxiety and anger among youth: 'It feels like we're living in a dystopia'
Description: In the wake of the third heatwave to hit France since May, frustration and anxiety have gripped part of the younger generations, faced with what some call 'the coolest summer of the rest of their lives.'

French 'libertines' rapes case: From livestreams to camp sites, where the sexual crimes took place
Description: Christophe B. and many other men he recruited are accused of raping women, with these sexual assaults broadcast online and in public. Responsibility is now being called into question. This is the final part of our investigation into the French 'libertines' rapes case.
Byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre,  Lorraine de Foucher  and  Morgane Tual

Title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
Description: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
Byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

Title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
Description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

Title: 'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
